rag_rules: Match annex owner to scope: feature-level contracts belong on a component type, while implementation-only bindings belong on a component implementation.
Do not place a type-owned annex that relies on subcomponent paths or other implementation-only names.
If an implementation-owned annex depends on subcomponents or connections, keep it after those declarations.
generic_rules: Insert the generated annex as one self-contained block under the selected owner and leave unrelated AADL structure unchanged.
Preserve existing features, subcomponents, connections, properties, end names, and declaration order while merging.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Keep the annex wrapper syntactically complete as a single unit: `annex agree {** ... **};`.
anti_patterns: Do not split one annex block across multiple owners or insert it inside feature, subcomponent, or connection declaration lists.
Do not rewrite clause internals, rename referenced identifiers, or move content between type and implementation scopes just to make insertion fit.
irrelevant_topics: Clause-level AGREE forms such as assume/guarantee/eq/const/assign
Requirement-driven range or dependency contract selection
Using AADL property association names or unit tokens as AGREE variables
